###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Libraries for running MS Cobol 4.5 under w2k

- **From:** Arnold Trembley <arnold.trembley@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2002-09-24T06:08:05+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D90011D.FD1680@worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<amncol$578$1@newsreader.mailgate.org> <217e491a.0209231547.621fb731@posting.google.com> <YSSj9.566545$UU1.93468@sccrnsc03>`

```
Jason wrote:
> 
> Richard,
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> thanks in advance

If you have a command line program in Windows NT that does not execute
(or does not execute correctly), you enter FORCEDOS on the command
line to put it into a more DOS-like environment.

For example, if you have a program named FOOBAR with command line
option /X, you normally enter "FOOBAR /X" on the command line to run
it.  To run it with FORCEDOS, you enter "FORCEDOS FOOBAR /X" on the
command line.

Depending on what exactly FOOBAR does, using FORCEDOS may allow it to
run (if it doesn't run at all), or may allow it run with fewer errors
than without FORCEDOS, or FORCEDOS may be no help at all.  But it's
certainly easy enough to try it out.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Libraries for running MS Cobol 4.5 under w2k

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-09-24T23:08:40-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0209242208.3160ea77@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<amncol$578$1@newsreader.mailgate.org> <217e491a.0209231547.621fb731@posting.google.com> <YSSj9.566545$UU1.93468@sccrnsc03> <L77k9.62883$jG2.3457546@bgtnsc05-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```
"Russell Styles" <RWSTYLES@worldnet.att.net> wrote 

>     I am not personally familar with that particular version, but some of
> the
> other versions (MF workbench 3.1 - an old 16 bit version, NOT NETX) had

MS Cobol 4.5 is derived from MF Workbench 2.5 - 1991
MS Cobol 5.0      "       "       "       3.0

> DOS only versions of RUN.EXE.  It had another extension, DLE if I remember
> correctly,
> and was installed only if you included the OS/2 stuff.  Just replace RUN.EXE
> (16K) with
> RUN.DLE (8K).

You are probably thinking of COBLIB.DLL and COBLIB.DLE which are the
shared runtime DLLs for OS/2 and DOS respectively.

The problem is actually the compiler: COBOL.EXE is a 'family'
application, as are several other .EXEs.  When it starts in OS/2 mode
it _needs_ to find the OS/2 COBLIB.DLL.  Just substituting the .DLE
won't work.
 
>     This assumes that you are using the INT/GNT runtime, not EXE's.

MS Cobol doesn't supply RUN.EXE to execute INT/GNT, it only produces
.OBJs which are linked to .EXEs for DOS, or .EXE and .DLLs for OS/2. 
These, however, may be 'int code' by specifying OPT"0".

You may note that with MF Workbench Cobol the RUN.EXE run-time for
INT/GNT is part of the OSX and requires a run-time licence to
distribute.  MS Cobol has no mechanism to collect run-time licences as
whatever can be built can be distributed freely.  This means that
[mostly] it can't build anything that MF would require a run-time
licence for.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Libraries for running MS Cobol 4.5 under w2k

- **From:** "Riccardo Aliani" <ralia2@tin.it>

```

occour for these operations).

Bye
Ric



"Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> ha scritto nel messaggio

```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Libraries for running MS Cobol 4.5 under w2k

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-09-24T12:51:15-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0209241151.4ff8dd55@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<amncol$578$1@newsreader.mailgate.org> <217e491a.0209231547.621fb731@posting.google.com> <ampd89$gue$1@newsreader.mailgate.org>`

```
"Riccardo Aliani" <ralia2@tin.it> wrote

> It works, thank you, but it's very strange. To compile I have to use
> Forcedos, to link or execute nothing (while the libraries it requires should
> occour for these operations).

The compiler is a 'family' application that can execute under DOS or
OS/2 1.x, but requires different DLLs depending on which it chooses. 
FORCEDOS hides the OS/2 facility in NT/W2K and thus makes the compiler
'choose' to run as a DOS program.

LINK is not a family application, it is DOS only.  The linker for OS/2
is a different program that is in the OS/2 SDK.

XM is also a DOS only program and thus using this to run your program
would ensure it ran as DOS even if you had managed to build it as a
family app (which is unlikely to be done accidentally).

There are a few other utilities in MS/MF Cobol that are family apps. 
Earlier versions got confused under some protected mode DOS
environments, such as Multiuser-DOS.
```

#### ↳ Re: Libraries for running MS Cobol 4.5 under w2k

- **From:** "Gael Wilson" <gael.wilson@nospam.microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-24T10:28:02+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ampbsp$38k$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`
- **References:** `<amncol$578$1@newsreader.mailgate.org>`

```
Riccardo,

The product you are using contains EXEs that run on both DOS and OS/2
(including the OS/2 subsystem on Windows NT/2000/XP). The default is for the
EXEs to use the OS/2 subsystem unless you use the FORCEDOS command mentioned
by a couple of other people. In order to use the OS/2 subsystem the
directory containing the COBOL system DLLs must be added to the Os2LibPath
environment variable otherwise you will get the error message you saw. Once
that is done no special commands are necessary to be able to run your
product.

"Riccardo Aliani" <ralia2@tin.it> wrote in message
news:amncol$578$1@newsreader.mailgate.org...
> Hi all,
>     I'm a teacher in an italian hi-grade school, and I'm using
> Microsoft-Microfocus Cobol 4.5 with my students since 1991. It works great
> under dos-w9x, but now we're upgrading to w2k professional. It doesn't
work,
> saying:
> Cobol runtime library not installed
> I think it needs a different runtime library for w2k/NT, but on
installation
> disk there's only the choice between DOS and OS/2. I've tried with OS/2,
no
> difference.
> Do you know any solution to this?
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

<a id="t-2cbe2cfc99"></a>
## Thread: Help request: Realia 16bit to 32bit conversion problem — 1 this month / 5 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

_Thread started [2002-08](2002-08.md#t-2cbe2cfc99)._

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help request: Realia 16bit to 32bit conversion problem

- **From:** maurice.northey@bigpond.com (Maurice Northey)
- **Date:** 2002-09-24T22:06:35-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<c825e98d.0209242106.42e2c455@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<697c3488.0208252243.3b3dcbbd@posting.google.com> <c8035ce7.0208260533.4a4829db@posting.google.com>`

```
> maurice.northey@team.telstra.com (Maurice Northey) wrote in message news:<697c3488.0208252243.3b3dcbbd@posting.google.com>...
> > I am converting a batch app from Realia Cobol v3 (DOS) to Realia
> > Workbench 3.1 (NT).
> > One program won't behave the same upon recompilation.
> > I have tried every trick in the book to find out why. 

For the benefit of anyone searching this message in the future, the
problem was that the following structure compiles OK but the program
appeared not to be able to access the elements of ABC-REC correctly.

01  ABC-REC.
  03  IN-CHAR OCCURS 1 TO 512
            DEPENDING ON REC-LEN
            INDEXED BY XIN
            PIC X.

The fix was to change ABC-REC to X(512).
```

---

<a id="t-ed984c6470"></a>
## Thread: Client / Server development from Windows to UniKix - How to do that ? — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Client / Server development from Windows to UniKix - How to do that ?

- **From:** "Panos Zotos" <pzotos@syntax.gr>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T10:40:48+03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<amrpdr$78i$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
Hi everybody

One of our customers wants to deploy a new application developed with MF
Cobol for Unix. This application runs under the control of UniKix on a AIX
server.

If anybody has some expertise on UniKix, I would appreciate some advice on
the following issue.

The customer wants to develop some additional  client / server applications
that will access UniKix transactions and programs from Windows environments.
He intends to use Net Express / Dialog System for Win32 Gui development, so
the question is how we get to invoke the UniKix transactions / programs. Can
standard ECI calls be applied to UniKix and, if yes, what software does he
need to do that (is there any CICS client for Windows supporting ECI calls)?

Thank you in advance for any response.

Panos
```

#### ↳ Re: Client / Server development from Windows to UniKix - How to do that ?

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T13:02:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3d91b207.769977@news.epix.net>`
- **References:** `<amrpdr$78i$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
"Panos Zotos" <pzotos@syntax.gr> wrote:

>Hi everybody
>
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
>Panos

Panos:

Jim Merkelt of the Business Assistance Group has developed a number of
routines which handle GUI screen display using NetExpress, UNIKIX and
COBOL sp2.  Jim's software is capable of handling both ECI and EPI
calls.

In addition, by adding the Thin Client his routines allow the GUI
screens to be displayed remotely across TCP/IP.  This satifies the
client/server requirement.

Contact Jim for more information.  You can reach him at the following
e-mail address.....PLEASE NOTE....in order to minimize spam for Jim, I
have "munged" his e-mail address.  Please correct the address before
sending a message to him.

You can reach Jim at "Jim Merkelt" <jmerkelt at comcat dot com>





Bob Wolfe
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
When replying by e-mail, make sure that you correct the e-mail address.
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Client / Server development from Windows to UniKix - How to do that ?

- **From:** "Changiz" <changizwNOMAIL@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T16:43:36-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<amt6pa0l67@enews3.newsguy.com>`
- **References:** `<amrpdr$78i$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
You may want to advise your customer not to do any new development using
Dialog System, as MF does not have any plans to enhance it in the future.
They are encouraging everyone to use VB for the user interface. There are
number of 3rd party add-ons that would do a better job for GUI.
```

## Thread: [HELP] Using DOS command in graphical cobol — 9 this month / 10 total

### [HELP] Using DOS command in graphical cobol

- **From:** "Pennywise" <angelsymphonyNO@SPAMlibero.it>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T09:47:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<KCfk9.8520$e31.185147@twister2.libero.it>`

```

Hello everybody, I use "Acubench" (by Acucorp) for developping in graphical
ambient. I must use a dos command (for exemple "del c:\programs\abc.txt")
and put it into my cobol program, so:


 - in working-storage section:

01   DEL-FILE  pic x(38) value "command.com /C del c:\programs\abc.txt".


- in procedure division:

call "system" using DEL-FILE
cancel "system"


This is OK: the program works, but I see a troublesome dos-window that
appears (over my graphical window) and disappears an half second later.. :-(
How can I remove this collateral result?
If I try to remove the string  "command.com /C" from working declarative,
dos command doesnt' work.

Is there anybody that can help me please?  :'-(
Tnx all!
```

#### ↳ Re: [HELP] Using DOS command in graphical cobol

- **From:** "Jason" <jasonsdog@att.net>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T16:41:03+00:00

- **References:** `<KCfk9.8520$e31.185147@twister2.libero.it>`

```
I don't know if this will work but try the command

"command.com /C del c:/programs\abc.txt >nul"

redirecting output to  nul   might hide the response message
```

#### ↳ Re: [HELP] Using DOS command in graphical cobol

- **From:** "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@kub.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-09-26T12:35:32+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<amunle$cqv$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`
- **References:** `<KCfk9.8520$e31.185147@twister2.libero.it>`

```
I don't know if your Acubench supports API calls (expect it though), cause
you could then you the DeleteFile call to remove the file. This means you
don't have to switch to a dos command prompt and remove the file using
windows. See the Microsoft SDK or www.all-api.net. It's more work to use the
Windows API, but it works much 'better' (aka 'visually nicer').

Rik
```

```
> cause you could then you the DeleteFile call to remove the file. This

Thanks!

```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: [HELP] Using DOS command in graphical cobol - This is the answer

- **From:** jmiedreich@sbcglobal.net (Jeremy Miedreich)
- **Date:** 2002-09-26T19:09:12-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<755320fc.0209261809.449b1058@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<KCfk9.8520$e31.185147@twister2.libero.it> <amunle$cqv$1@mailnews.kub.nl> <1zCk9.13726$e31.284519@twister2.libero.it>`

```
Well i once was troubled with the same problem using Acucobol...this
will solve your dos window problem - In version 4.3 and up maybe in
lower versions as well, use this line

CALL "C$SYSTEM" USING "COMMNAD LINE ARGUMENTS", 32.

I always like to using a pre-written batach file to be called in the
command line arguments.  This is in the documentation.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: [HELP] Using DOS command in graphical cobol - This is the answer

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Pennywise" <angelsymphonyNO@SPAMlibero.it>
- **Date:** 2002-09-27T06:59:02+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<WkTk9.16984$e31.364332@twister2.libero.it>`
- **References:** `<KCfk9.8520$e31.185147@twister2.libero.it> <amunle$cqv$1@mailnews.kub.nl> <1zCk9.13726$e31.284519@twister2.libero.it> <755320fc.0209261809.449b1058@posting.google.com>`

```

> CALL "C$SYSTEM" USING "COMMNAD LINE ARGUMENTS", 32.

Fantastic!!! Thanks!!! Yesterday I tryed "call c$system", but without ", 32"
it didn't work. Now it's perfect!!
Bye!
```

## Thread: recommend a compiler — 4 this month / 10 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### recommend a compiler

- **From:** Allyn Uptain <uptain@digitalexp.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T15:24:44-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<c164pu44g4s9eelb24cle0a4slclbmspsu@4ax.com>`

```
Hi All,

I need to buy a Windows Cobol compiler and I sure would appreciate your advice, suggestions or comments.

Compatibility is a non-issue.  I have a DOS application written in RPG II.  I need to get it rewritten in native Windows.  I've been playing with VB for the last few months.  I can't handle it.  Hence, I'm looking for a good Cobol compiler that doesn't have run time fees.  Or at least minimal run time fees.

Gui is unimportant to me.  This is an accounting application.  This is a data entry application.  This is a run-your-business application.  This is a point-of-sale application.  Those guys don't want to mouse around.  They just want to use the keyboard & printer & have their A/R updated.

I know I can get a Windows based RPG, but how long will it be supported?  I figure Cobol has a longer future on the PC.

If you're a vendor of a PC Cobol, feel free to email your pricing structures to me at
uptain  at  digitalexp.com

thanks
allyn uptain
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: recommend a compiler

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-09-30T22:55:32-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0209302155.45507bb@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<c164pu44g4s9eelb24cle0a4slclbmspsu@4ax.com> <3D9871C5.CE79FC62@attglobal.net> <217e491a.0209301732.6e504c19@posting.google.com> <Dy7m9.45472$8o3.1196241@twister.austin.rr.com>`

```
"Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@hot.NOSPAM.rr.com> wrote

> Is this new? The versions I have of Fujitsu 3 don't have any disclaimers about academic licenses or anything. The base compiler
> appears to be free forever. The 'Professional' and 'Enterprise' features are only trial versions though.

From the Web site where it is downlaoded:

>> For those of you who are learning COBOL on your own or taking a
COBOL
>> class and need a COBOL compiler to experiment with - but NOT create
>> production applications - we provide Fujitsu COBOL Version 3 for
FREE.
and:

>> To receive your free student version, simply fill out the short 
>> registration form and download the software. 

It may be that you didn't get your version 3 by downloading, I paid
for version 3 Enterprise CDs. I would expect that I can use this to
distribute forever too (though I also have later versions).  I don't
know what terms and conditions apply to the versions in the books, I
suspect that they don't have the run-time installer.

However, if someone downloads the student version 3 it quite clearly
states the conditions under which it is supplied to them for free.  It
would seem that registering, which should be done before downloading,
is an agreement to these terms.
```

<a id="t-fb6a2446ed"></a>
## Thread: isam data files — 10 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### isam data files

- **From:** "Jason" <jasonsdog@att.net>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T20:25:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bZok9.681$wH.60@sccrnsc01>`

```
Hello all,

I have used for years an old DOS compiler to create and use ISAM files.

Recently got Net Express and have been benchmarking performance and found
file i-o REALLY SLOW.

For example I can create with DOS compiler a 100,000 record isam file in
about 10 seconds
using NetExpress it takes 150 seconds

should I go to a third party file system to get faster i-o ?

any suggestions greatly appreciated


TIA,


J.W.
```

#### ↳ Re: isam data files

- **From:** "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@hot.NOSPAM.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-26T01:56:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<vPtk9.13809$8o3.304873@twister.austin.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<bZok9.681$wH.60@sccrnsc01>`

```
Roughly speaking - You Got Something Wrong There Son.

To create a 100000 record indexed file under Windows XP (1.8ghz P4):
   AcuCobol  -  about 0.2 seconds
   MicroFocus NetExpress - 1.1 seconds
   IBM VisualAge COBOL 3.1 - 1.9 seconds / .7 seconds (BTRIEVE)
   Fujitsu COBOL 3 -- ~ 2.1 seconds

Under AIX on a modest RS/6000:
   MicroFocus - 8 seconds      (no cache)
   IBM COBOL - 11 seconds (no cache)
   Microfocus  - .3 seconds      (cache)
   IBM COBOL - .2 seconds   (cache)


Of course, Windows does cache.  The file created was six characters in length and used
the record number as the key.

-Paul


"Jason" <jasonsdog@att.net> wrote in message news:bZok9.681$wH.60@sccrnsc01...
> Hello all,
>
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: isam data files

- **From:** "Jason" <jasonsdog@att.net>
- **Date:** 2002-09-26T04:08:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<WKvk9.77863$gA4.27485@sccrnsc02>`
- **References:** `<bZok9.681$wH.60@sccrnsc01> <vPtk9.13809$8o3.304873@twister.austin.rr.com>`

```

Raul,

below is the source of my benchmark

would you, could you  ISAM-I-AM     compile and see if you don't get a slow
time ?


thanks again for your help


JW





  IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID.   BENCH1.

********  TEST ISAM FILE PERFORMANCE ON WRITING

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT FILEONE ASSIGN TO "TESTTIME.DAT"
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS IS RANDOM
               RECORD KEY IS TEST-KEY
               ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS TF-FIELD3 WITH DUPLICATES
               STATUS IS TEST-CODE.

       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  FILEONE.
       01  TEST-REC.
           03  TEST-KEY.
               05  TF-FIELD1        PIC X(20).
               05  TF-FIELD2        PIC X(16).
               05  TF-FIELD3        PIC X(11).
               05  TF-FIELD4        PIC X(15).
               05  TF-FIELD5        PIC X(25).
           03  TF-FIELD8            PIC X(144).

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01  TEST-CODE    PIC XX.
       01  WS-FIELD3    PIC 9(12) VALUE 0.
       01  WS-FIELD2    PIC 9(11) VALUE 0.
       01  SUBA         PIC 9(7) VALUE 0.
       01  SUBB         PIC 9(7) VALUE 0.
       01  SUBC         PIC 9(7) VALUE 0.
       01  SUBD         PIC 9(7) VALUE 0.
       01  WS-TIME-START     PIC S9(8).
       01  WS-TIME-STOP      PIC S9(8).
       01  WS-BLANK-SCREEN    PIC X(1920) VALUE SPACES.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.

       0000-RECORD-PROMPT.
           DISPLAY "HOW MANY RECORDS TO CREATE FOR THIS TEST "
               LINE 1 POSITION 1 ERASE.
           ACCEPT SUBC LINE 1 POSITION 43.
           IF SUBC = 0 STOP RUN.

           ACCEPT WS-TIME-START FROM TIME.
       0001-OPEN-FILES.
           OPEN OUTPUT FILEONE.
           DISPLAY "RECORDS MADE" LINE 1 POSITION 15 ERASE.
           DISPLAY SUBC LINE 2 POSITION 43.
           DISPLAY " REQUESTED" LINE 2 POSITION 60.
       0002-CREATE-RECORDS.
           INITIALIZE TEST-REC.
           ADD 1 TO WS-FIELD3.
           IF WS-FIELD3 > SUBC GO TO 0003-STOP.
           DIVIDE WS-FIELD3 BY 50 GIVING SUBD.
           MOVE SUBD TO TF-FIELD1.
           ADD 1 TO WS-FIELD2.
           DIVIDE WS-FIELD2 BY 10 GIVING SUBA REMAINDER SUBB.
           ADD 1000000 TO SUBD.
           MOVE SUBD TO TF-FIELD3.
           MOVE WS-FIELD3 TO TF-FIELD4.
           WRITE TEST-REC INVALID KEY GO TO ERROR-PARA.
           DISPLAY WS-FIELD3 LINE 1 POSITION 1.
           GO TO 0002-CREATE-RECORDS.

       ERROR-PARA.
           DISPLAY "ERROR AT "   LINE 5  POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY TEST-CODE     LINE 6  POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY " = FILE STATUS RETURNED" LINE 6 POSITION 4.
           DISPLAY WS-FIELD3     LINE 7  POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY SUBA          LINE 8  POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY SUBB          LINE 9  POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY SUBC          LINE 10 POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY SUBD          LINE 11 POSITION 1.

       0003-STOP.
           CLOSE FILEONE.
           ACCEPT WS-TIME-STOP FROM TIME.
           DISPLAY WS-TIME-START LINE 15 POSITION 1.
           DISPLAY WS-TIME-STOP LINE 16 POSITION 1.
           STOP RUN.

"Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@hot.NOSPAM.rr.com> wrote in message
news:vPtk9.13809$8o3.304873@twister.austin.rr.com...
> Roughly speaking - You Got Something Wrong There Son.
>
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> Of course, Windows does cache.  The file created was six characters in
length and used
> the record number as the key.
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> "Jason" <jasonsdog@att.net> wrote in message
news:bZok9.681$wH.60@sccrnsc01...
> > Hello all,
> >
> > I have used for years an old DOS compiler to create and use ISAM files.
> >
> > Recently got Net Express and have been benchmarking performance and
found
> > file i-o REALLY SLOW.
> >
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

```
    Turns out that what you are really testing here is
the display verb.  Net express's ADIS subsystem
is REALLY REALLY slow.

    Try displaying the record number only every 100
writes instead of every write.  That will even things out.
```

```
"Jason" <jasonsdog@att.net> wrote in message
news:bZok9.681$wH.60@sccrnsc01...
> Hello all,
>
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
>

    Could it be that you switched from using DOS O/S to windows?
The "lock mode" in the select statement can make a HUGE
difference when on a network (windows is always on a "network"
of sorts.

    If you still have the DOS PC around, try running with and without SHARE.
You might be shocked at the difference with "LOCK MODE MANUAL".

    "LOCK MODE EXCLUSIVE" is fast, as is no lock mode at all.

    This is most obvious with small records.
```

<a id="t-70912fec74"></a>
## Thread: Dynamic Allocation — 4 msgs

### Dynamic Allocation

- **From:** "Giulio Belrango" <giuliobelrango@rodgers.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T21:21:56+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<UNpk9.51956$q41.32655@news02.bloor.is.net.cable.rogers.com>`

```
Does anyone have an example using COBOL showing how to dynamically allocate
files ie without JCL.

Thanks...
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic Allocation

- **From:** "Charles Hottel" <chottel@cpcug.org>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T23:47:49+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01c264ed$9f895700$dc8bf243@chottel>`
- **References:** `<UNpk9.51956$q41.32655@news02.bloor.is.net.cable.rogers.com>`

```
The example below use environment variables.  It works as is on my system. 
You may have to use lowercase for "putenv" .  Use googlr to search for more
info as this had been discussed before.

//JOBNAME JOB (ACCT,'ROOM-NBR'),'HOTTEL COBDYNAL',MSGCLASS=S,CLASS=K       
    
//PROCLIB JCLLIB ORDER=USERID.DVL.PROC                                     
    
//        SET TITLE1='XXX.COB00005'                                        
    
//OUTPUT INCLUDE MEMBER=DESTCH                                             
    
//*-------------------------------------------------------------------*    
    
//*        COBOL II COMPILE                                           *    
    
//*-------------------------------------------------------------------*    
    
//STEP01   EXEC  COB3CLG,LIBRARY='DVL.SOURCE.LIBMAST',PROG=IEFBR14,        
    
//    LOPT='AMODE=31,RMODE=ANY'                                            
    
//COMP.SYSIN DD  *                                                         
    
       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.                                            
    
       PROGRAM-ID. COBDYNAL.                                               
    
      *===============================================================*    
    
      * This program dynamically allocates and deallocates a file          
    
      * using environment variables.      No DD statements for the         
    
      * file sould appear in the JCL:                                      
    
      *                                                                    
    
      * Needs COBOL 2.2.0                                                  
    
      * Compile with NODYNAM.  CALL must be static.                        
    
      *                                                                    
    
      * PUTENV is a C function that creates an environment variable.       
    
      * See C/C++ Run-Time Library Reference (SC28-1663)                   
    
      *                                                                    
    
      * Language Environment Programming Reference (SC26-3312)             
    
      *                                                                    
    
      * COBOL Language Reference (SC26-9046-04)                            
    
      *                                                                    
    
      *===============================================================*    
    
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.                                               
    
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.                                              
    
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.                                               
    
       FILE-CONTROL.                                                       
    
           SELECT FILEIN ASSIGN  UT-S-DYNFILE                              
    
                  FILE STATUS IS FILEIN-STATUS.                            
    
      /                                                                    
    
       DATA DIVISION.                                                      
    
       FILE SECTION.                                                       
    
       FD  FILEIN                                                          
    
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS                                   
    
           RECORDING MODE IS F                                             
    
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD                                      
    
           BLOCK CONTAINS 0 CHARACTERS                                     
    
           DATA RECORD IS FILEIN-REC.                                      
    
       01  FILEIN-REC.                                                     
    
           05 FILLER             PIC X(80).                                
    
                                                                           
    
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.                                            
    
       01  FILEIN-STATUS         PIC 9(02).                                
    
       01  RC                    PIC 9(9) BINARY.                          
    
       01  ADDRESS-POINTER       POINTER.                                  
    
       01  FILE-ENVIRONMENT-VARIABLE PIC X(31)                             
    
           VALUE 'DYNFILE=DSN(USERID.TEMP80) SHR'.                         
    
      *                                                                    
    
       01  INPUT-REC             PIC X(80).                                
    
       01  IN-COUNT              PIC 9(9)   BINARY VALUE 0.                
    
      /                                                                    
    
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.                                                 
    
       INITIAL-STUFF.                                                      
    
                                                                           
    
***********SET ADDRESS-POINTER TO ADDRESS OF                               
    
************** FILE-ENVIRONMENT-VARIABLE.                                  
    
           CALL "SETPTO" USING ADDRESS-POINTER                             
    
                               FILE-ENVIRONMENT-VARIABLE.                  
    
                                                                           
    
           CALL "PUTENV" USING BY VALUE ADDRESS-POINTER                    
    
               RETURNING RC.                                               
    
           IF RC NOT = ZERO                                                
    
               DISPLAY 'PUTENV FAILED'                                     
    
               GOBACK                                                      
    
           END-IF.                                                         
    
                                                                           
    
           OPEN INPUT FILEIN.                                              
    
                                                                           
    
       READER.                                                             
    
           READ FILEIN INTO INPUT-REC,                                     
    
               AT END GO TO E-O-J.                                         
    
           ADD +1 TO IN-COUNT.                                             
    
           DISPLAY FILEIN-REC.                                             
    
           GO TO READER.                                                   
    
                                                                           
    
       E-O-J.                                                              
    
           CLOSE FILEIN.                                                   
    
           GOBACK.                                                         
    
//LKED.SYSLIB  DD                                                          
    
//             DD                                                          
    
//             DD DSN=PREFIX.DVL.BATLOAD,DISP=SHR                          
    
//LKED.SYSLMOD DD DSN=&&LOADLIB(COBDYNAL),DISP=(OLD,PASS)                  
    
//LKED.SYSIN DD *                                                          
    
  NAME COBDYNAL(R)                                                         
    
//GO.SYSUDUMP DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSPRINT DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSOUT   DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSDBOUT DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    




Giulio Belrango <giuliobelrango@rodgers.com> wrote in article
<UNpk9.51956$q41.32655@news02.bloor.is.net.cable.rogers.com>...
> Does anyone have an example using COBOL showing how to dynamically
allocate
> files ie without JCL.
> 
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic Allocation

- **From:** "Charles Hottel" <chottel@cpcug.org>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T23:49:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01c264ed$ca85fa80$dc8bf243@chottel>`
- **References:** `<UNpk9.51956$q41.32655@news02.bloor.is.net.cable.rogers.com>`

```
The example below uses BPXWDYN.

//JOBNAME JOB (ACCT,'ROOM-NBR'),'HOTTEL COBDYNAM',MSGCLASS=S,CLASS=K       
    
//PROCLIB JCLLIB ORDER=USERID.DVL.PROC                                     
    
//        SET TITLE1='XXX.COB00006'                                        
    
//OUTPUT INCLUDE MEMBER=DESTCH                                             
    
//*-------------------------------------------------------------------*    
    
//*        COBOL II COMPILE                                           *    
    
//*-------------------------------------------------------------------*    
    
//STEP01   EXEC  COB3CLG,LIBRARY='DVL.SOURCE.LIBMAST',PROG=IEFBR14,        
    
//    LOPT='AMODE=31,RMODE=ANY'                                            
    
//COMP.SYSIN DD  *                                                         
    
       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.                                            
    
       PROGRAM-ID. COBDYNAM.                                               
    
      *===============================================================*    
    
      * This program dynamically allocates and deallocates a file          
    
      * using the BPXWDYN IBM subroutine. No DD statements for the         
    
      * file sould appear in the JCL:                                      
    
      *===============================================================*    
    
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.                                               
    
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.                                              
    
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.                                               
    
       FILE-CONTROL.                                                       
    
           SELECT FILEIN ASSIGN TO FILEIN.                                 
    
      /                                                                    
    
       DATA DIVISION.                                                      
    
       FILE SECTION.                                                       
    
       FD  FILEIN                                                          
    
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS                                   
    
           RECORDING MODE IS F                                             
    
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD                                      
    
           BLOCK CONTAINS 0 CHARACTERS                                     
    
           DATA RECORD IS FILEIN-REC.                                      
    
       01  FILEIN-REC.                                                     
    
           05 FILLER             PIC X(80).                                
    
                                                                           
    
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.                                            
    
       01  INPUT-REC             PIC X(80).                                
    
       01  IN-COUNT              PIC 9(9)   BINARY VALUE 0.                
    
       01  SUB-PGM               PIC X(8)   VALUE 'BPXWDYN '.              
    
       01  ALLOC-CMD.                                                      
    
           05  FILLER            PIC X(50)  VALUE                          
    
               ' ALLOC DD(FILEIN) DSN(USERID.TEMP80) MSG(WTP) OLD'.        
    
           05  FILLER            PIC X(50)  VALUE                          
    
               '                                                  '.       
    
                                                                           
    
       01 COMMAND-STRING.                                                  
    
           05 C-S-LEN            PIC S9(4)   BINARY VALUE +100.            
    
           05 C-S-DATA.                                                    
    
               07 FILLER         PIC X(100).                               
    
      /                                                                    
    
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.                                                 
    
       INITIAL-STUFF.                                                      
    
                                                                           
    
           MOVE ALLOC-CMD TO C-S-DATA.                                     
    
           CALL SUB-PGM USING COMMAND-STRING.                              
    
           DISPLAY 'AFTER ALLOC RC = ' RETURN-CODE.                        
    
                                                                           
    
           OPEN INPUT FILEIN.                                              
    
                                                                           
    
       READER.                                                             
    
           READ FILEIN INTO INPUT-REC,                                     
    
               AT END GO TO E-O-J.                                         
    
           ADD +1 TO IN-COUNT.                                             
    
           DISPLAY FILEIN-REC.                                             
    
           GO TO READER.                                                   
    
                                                                           
    
       E-O-J.                                                              
    
           CLOSE FILEIN.                                                   
    
           MOVE 'FREE DD(FILEIN)' TO C-S-DATA.                             
    
           CALL SUB-PGM USING COMMAND-STRING.                              
    
           DISPLAY 'AFTER FREE RC = ' RETURN-CODE.                         
    
           GOBACK.                                                         
    
//LKED.SYSLIB  DD                                                          
    
//             DD                                                          
    
//             DD DSN=PREFIX.DVL.BATLOAD,DISP=SHR                          
    
//LKED.SYSLMOD DD DSN=&&LOADLIB(COBDYN),DISP=(OLD,PASS)                    
    
//LKED.SYSIN DD *                                                          
    
  NAME COBDYN(R)                                                           
    
//GO.SYSUDUMP DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSPRINT DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSOUT   DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSDBOUT DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    




Giulio Belrango <giuliobelrango@rodgers.com> wrote in article
<UNpk9.51956$q41.32655@news02.bloor.is.net.cable.rogers.com>...
> Does anyone have an example using COBOL showing how to dynamically
allocate
> files ie without JCL.
> 
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic Allocation

- **From:** "Charles Hottel" <chottel@cpcug.org>
- **Date:** 2002-09-25T23:49:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01c264ed$e9b0cca0$dc8bf243@chottel>`
- **References:** `<UNpk9.51956$q41.32655@news02.bloor.is.net.cable.rogers.com>`

```
The example below usea batch TSO.

/JOBNAME JOB (ACCT,'ROOM-NBR'),'HOTTEL COBTSO',MSGCLASS=S,CLASS=K          
   
//PROCLIB JCLLIB ORDER=USERID.DVL.PROC                                     
    
//        SET TITLE1='XXX.COB00007'                                        
    
//OUTPUT INCLUDE MEMBER=DESTCH                                             
    
//*-------------------------------------------------------------------*    
    
//*        COBOL II COMPILE                                           *    
    
//*-------------------------------------------------------------------*    
    
//STEP01   EXEC  COB3CLG,LIBRARY='DVL.SOURCE.LIBMAST',PROG=IEFBR14,        
    
//    OPT=DYNAM,LOPT='AMODE=31,RMODE=ANY'                                  
    
//COMP.SYSIN DD  *                                                         
    
       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.                                            
    
         PROGRAM-ID. COBTSO.                                               
    
      ******************************************************************   
    
      *                                                                *   
    
      *   MODULE NAME = COBTSO                                         *   
    
      *                                                                *   
    
      *   DESCRIPTIVE NAME = ISSUE TSO COMMANDS FROM A COBOL PROGRAM.  *   
    
      *                                                                *   
    
      *   FUNCTION = THIS SAMPLE PROGRAM DEMONSTRATES HOW TO INVOKE    *   
    
      *              TSO COMMANDS FROM A COBOL PROGRAM USING           *   
    
      *              STANDARD TSO SERVICES AS DOCUMENTED IN THE        *   
    
      *              TSO/E PROGRAMMING SERVICES MANUAL.                *   
    
      *                                                                *   
    
      *              MOST TSO COMMANDS, INCLUDING CLISTS AND REXX      *   
    
      *              EXECS CAN BE EXECUTED USING THIS TECHNIQUE.       *   
    
      *              TSO COMMANDS WHICH REQUIRE AUTHORIZATION          *   
    
      *              (SUCH AS OUTPUT, SEND, TRANSMIT AND RECEIVE)      *   
    
      *              WILL NOT WORK.                                    *   
    
      *                                                                *   
    
      *   AUTHOR   =  GILBERT SAINT-FLOUR <GSF@POBOX.COM>              *   
    
      *                                                                *   
    
      ******************************************************************   
    
      /                                                                    
    
       DATA DIVISION.                                                      
    
        WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.                                           
    
         01 FILLER.                                                        
    
           05 WS-DUMMY        PIC S9(8) COMP.                              
    
           05 WS-RETURN-CODE  PIC S9(8) COMP.                              
    
           05 WS-REASON-CODE  PIC S9(8) COMP.                              
    
           05 WS-INFO-CODE    PIC S9(8) COMP.                              
    
           05 WS-CPPL-ADDRESS PIC S9(8) COMP.                              
    
           05 WS-FLAGS        PIC X(4) VALUE X'00010001'.                  
    
           05 WS-BUFFER       PIC X(256).                                  
    
           05 WS-LENGTH       PIC S9(8) COMP VALUE 256.                    
    
      /                                                                    
    
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.                                                 
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
      *          CALL IKJTSOEV TO CREATE THE TSO/E ENVIRONMENT         *   
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
           CALL 'IKJTSOEV' USING WS-DUMMY                                  
    
                                 WS-RETURN-CODE                            
    
                                 WS-REASON-CODE                            
    
                                 WS-INFO-CODE                              
    
                                 WS-CPPL-ADDRESS.                          
    
           IF WS-RETURN-CODE > ZERO                                        
    
             DISPLAY 'IKJTSOEV FAILED, RETURN-CODE=' WS-RETURN-CODE        
    
                                     ' REASON-CODE=' WS-REASON-CODE        
    
                                     'INFO-CODE='    WS-INFO-CODE          
    
             MOVE WS-RETURN-CODE TO RETURN-CODE                            
    
             STOP RUN.                                                     
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
      *          BUILD THE TSO/E COMMAND IN WS-BUFFER                  *   
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
                                                                           
    
           MOVE 'ALLOCATE DD(SYSPUNCH) SYSOUT HOLD' TO WS-BUFFER.          
    
                                                                           
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
      *   CALL THE TSO/E SERVICE ROUTINE TO EXECUTE THE TSO/E COMMAND  *   
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
           CALL 'IKJEFTSR' USING WS-FLAGS                                  
    
                                 WS-BUFFER                                 
    
                                 WS-LENGTH                                 
    
                                 WS-RETURN-CODE                            
    
                                 WS-REASON-CODE                            
    
                                 WS-DUMMY.                                 
    
           IF WS-RETURN-CODE > ZERO                                        
    
             DISPLAY 'IKJEFTSR FAILED, RETURN-CODE=' WS-RETURN-CODE        
    
                                     ' REASON-CODE=' WS-REASON-CODE        
    
             MOVE WS-RETURN-CODE TO RETURN-CODE                            
    
             STOP RUN.                                                     
    
                                                                           
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
      *          CHECK THAT THE ALLOCATE COMMAND WORKED                *   
    
      *----------------------------------------------------------------*   
    
           DISPLAY 'ALLOCATE WORKED ! ' UPON SYSPUNCH.                     
    
                                                                           
    
           STOP RUN.                                                       
    
//LKED.SYSLIB  DD                                                          
    
//             DD                                                          
    
//             DD DSN=PREFIX.DVL.BATLOAD,DISP=SHR                          
    
//LKED.SYSLMOD DD DSN=&&LOADLIB(COBTSO),DISP=(OLD,PASS)                    
    
//LKED.SYSIN DD *                                                          
    
  NAME COBTSO(R)                                                           
    
//GO.SYSUDUMP DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSPRINT DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSOUT   DD SYSOUT=*                                                  
    
//GO.SYSDBOUT DD SYSOUT=*
```

```
Hello Gurus, Please HELP

I am doing the following things.

I have created a project in NE 3.1 and I have 3 programs. Say Program A,
B and C.  Program A Calls B and inturn calls C.

I also added a Class say ClassD (with DLL option ) to the project and In
one of the methods say Meth1, I am calling program A. I have registered
the ClassD (DLL).

Then I have a VB front End. I am creating an instance of ClassD and
trying to access this method Meth1. I am getting an error that Program A
is not in current directory or drive.

Is the above possible in first case? If so, what have I done wrong?

Thanks in Advance.

Babu
```

```
references
```

## Thread: Cobol Jobs in Netherlands??? — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Cobol Jobs in Netherlands???

- **From:** Bud4life247@hotmail.com (Bud4life)
- **Date:** 2002-09-26T21:30:05-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<4a055f2d.0209262030.1d9c5a7@posting.google.com>`

```
Are there any COBOL jobs in Netherlands??

Please post your comments/opinions about IT Industry/COBOL here. 

Thanks
Bud.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol Jobs in Netherlands???

- **From:** "Werner Spreeuwenberg" <wspreeuw@wanadoo.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-09-30T21:26:03+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Lj3m9.20962$PO2.261876@castor.casema.net>`
- **References:** `<4a055f2d.0209262030.1d9c5a7@posting.google.com>`

```
I've been looking around in the mainframe sector for coolgen/cobol and have
been aware that cobol on it's own (or in connection with DB2/CICS) does not
have a market momentarily in Holland. SAP/SAS/ORACLE is momentarily high in
demand.

Not only is there no jobs, tariffs are VERY low and only preferred suppliers
(i.g. CMG/Roccade) get the contracts. Roccade has also recently flooded the
market by putting off 350 cobol programmers.

You can always try the site of the inland revenue office in Apeldoorn.
(www.belastingdienst.nl)

Good luck

Werner
```

<a id="t-cea49df1b8"></a>
## Thread: iteration using level 88 range-vars — 2 msgs

### iteration using level 88 range-vars

- **From:** "Torsten Reichert" <Thorsten.Reichert@dresdner-bank.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-27T10:31:17+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<an140j$cus1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
Hi everybody

Does anyone know a solution for the displaying all valid values of a split
range ?

05    RANGE-VAR-88        PIC S9(02).
        ...
        88  RANGE-A    VALUE     1 THRU  8
                                                   41 THRU 45.
        ...


    SET   RANGE-A                  TO TRUE
    MOVE RANGE-VAR-88     TO COND-VAR

    PERFORM UNTIL ???????
          DISPLAY COND-VAR
    END-PERFORM

Thank you
```

#### ↳ Re: iteration using level 88 range-vars

- **From:** Volker Bandke <vbandke@bsp-gmbh.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-27T12:22:54+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<j7c8pukjc9j6iag3d8booqpt10ntuss2f7@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<an140j$cus1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
PERFORM VARYING I FROM -99 BY 1 UNTIL I > +99
   MOVE I TO RANGE-VAR-88
   IF RANGE-A THEN
      DISPLAY I
   END-IF
END-PERFORM



And now go and make the rest of your Homework
      
      
      
      
                                                            
     With kind Regards            |\      _,,,---,,_
```

## Thread: Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access — 3 msgs

### Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access

- **From:** "AIeX" <fearless@ono.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-28T13:58:05+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Nzil9.4133$Dr2.363931@news.ono.com>`

```
Hello, anybody knows how to access to Microsoft Access databases from cobol
aplications. I need to do this, please if anybody knows how to do that
please reply this post or send me an e-mail.

Thanks,

Juan
fearless@ono.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access

- **From:** "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@kub.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-09-28T16:19:29+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<an4dh4$cng$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`
- **References:** `<Nzil9.4133$Dr2.363931@news.ono.com>`

```
Use ODBC. Most Windows-based COBOL compilers support them. For NetCOBOL I
know for sure it's supported.
```

#### ↳ Re: Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access

- **From:** lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca
- **Date:** 2002-09-28T14:22:05+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-2ojsBpVVzLFw@h24-82-204-17.wp.shawcable.net>`
- **References:** `<Nzil9.4133$Dr2.363931@news.ono.com>`

```
On Sat, 28 Sep 2002 14:58:05 UTC, "AIeX" <fearless@ono.com> wrote:

> Hello, anybody knows how to access to Microsoft Access databases from cobol
> aplications. I need to do this, please if anybody knows how to do that
> please reply this post or send me an e-mail.
> 
> Thanks,

MS Access can be accessed through an ODBC driver. You can do this in 
COBOL by calling the appropriate ODBC APIs or if you have Netexpress 
COBOL you can use embedded SQL with the DBMAN=ODBC (I think that is it
anyway) compiler directive.
```

---

<a id="t-f516332c27"></a>
## Thread: Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access — 2 msgs

### Re: Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-09-28T15:47:09+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1akl9.458$XX3.326263@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<CAil9.4134$Dr2.363384@news.ono.com>`

```
That would depend (a lot) on your particular brand compiler (MF, AccuCobol,
etc) and your operating system (MS-DOS, Windows, Unix).

I think "Microsoft Cobol" was only offered in an MS-DOS version, and only a
very long time ago.  I am not aware of any software designed specifically to
read/write Access(r) databases from MS-DOS, so that would leave you only the
option of calling a Windows program from your DOS program.

Or, I guess,  figure out the structure of the *.dbm files and  try to read
those as a stream-type input.

That is, if there is no canned software which can read/write Access Db's
from MS-DOS, you are basically SOL.
```

#### ↳ Re: Microsoft Cobol and Microsoft Access

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-09-28T23:09:21-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0209282209.46813e3d@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<CAil9.4134$Dr2.363384@news.ono.com> <1akl9.458$XX3.326263@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>`

```
"Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net> wrote 

> I think "Microsoft Cobol" was only offered in an MS-DOS version, and only a
> very long time ago.  

MS Cobol 4.5 (1991) is equivalent to MF Cobol 2.5 and will create DOS,
OS/2 1.x and Windows 3.1x programs.  MS Cobol 5.0 (1992) is == MF
Cobol 3.0 and will do DOS and Windows 3.1 programs, the ability to do
OS/2 1.x was crippled by MS.

I have written full Windows API graphics programs in MS Cobol 4.5 and
5.0 (later in MF 3.4), ie drawing graphics in several windows and
drag&drop etc.
```

---

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### JCL Monitoring

- **From:** starclipper01@hotmail.com (AW)
- **Date:** 2002-09-28T16:49:33-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<599cfa53.0209281549.294df4ed@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi,

Does anyone know how to set up some lines of code where if a job
abends it can notify me via cellphone?  Or point me to a site that
teaches how to do this?

Thanks.
```

#### ↳ Re: JCL Monitoring

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-29T12:04:08+03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cecdpu4ol5uvi7ghh496m5obvsrv7185ol@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<599cfa53.0209281549.294df4ed@posting.google.com>`

```
On 28 Sep 2002 16:49:33 -0700 starclipper01@hotmail.com (AW) wrote:

:>Does anyone know how to set up some lines of code where if a job
:>abends it can notify me via cellphone?  Or point me to a site that
:>teaches how to do this?

A COBOL solution?

Well, I guess you could write a COBOL program that executed COND=ONLY which
opens A SMTP session to send a message to your pager/cell phone.

Oh, by the way, there are auto-operations products that provide this solution
and would even handle the case of JCL errors like 'dataset not found'.
```

<a id="t-6bd24d57ff"></a>
## Thread: TCP Client Sample Code with Winsock — 4 this month / 21 total

### TCP Client Sample Code with Winsock

- **From:** "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@hot.NOSPAM.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-29T22:21:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<A1Ll9.40149$8o3.981815@twister.austin.rr.com>`

```
Argh!  I started out to do something I thought was very simple, and it has turned into a bloody nightmare! 
From a COBOL program, I need to open up a socket, read a webpage, parse the information on the webpage, and construct a display from it. (Not an HTML display, a console based display.) 

Using NetExpress and Fujitsu-3, I have been able to open the doggon socket, but I cannot, for the life of me, seem to get the buffer to send the right size command to the remote web site. Either it will give no reply at all, or by upping the size of the buffer, I can cause a 414 error on the WebServer and get back a nice reply telling me the URI is too long. (At least I know the doggon thing works! :) 

This has to be from Windows I am afraid, so does anyone out there have sample code to do this, or 
even non-sample code they would be willing to share? 

The program opens a socket, sets the connection to port 80 of the remote web server (in this case 205.238.189.192) and issues a command. It then reads the socket until the return code from the receive call is 0. The point that seems to be driving me crazy is this: 


 000277        Send-Data SECTION.
 000278            call win32api "send" using
 000279                    by value SOCKET
 000280                    by reference SendBuf
 000281                    by value CmdSize
 000282                    by value 0  size 4
 000283                    returning WS-Return-Code
 000284            end-call

The CmdSize argument is currently set to the size of the command going out, with a X'0A0D0A0D' 
appended to it. In this particular case the command is: 

GET / HTTP1/.0

And the command size is set to 18.  

Help? 

Thanks 
-Paul
```

#### ↳ Re: TCP Client Sample Code with Winsock

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-09-29T18:51:13-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0209291751.67d06170@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<A1Ll9.40149$8o3.981815@twister.austin.rr.com>`

```
"Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@hot.NOSPAM.rr.com> wrote

> From a COBOL program, I need to open up a socket, read a webpage, parse 
> the information on the webpage, and construct a display from it. (Not an 
> HTML display, a console based display.) 

I do that using Python.  Python has a nice HTML parser class that
makes it quite easy to fetch a page and extract the data.  Use a
WinExec to fire off the Python program and have it write the a results
text file that the Cobol can pick up the data from.
```

```
out what is going wrong.

-Paul
```

#### ↳ Re: TCP Client Sample Code with Winsock

- **From:** dashwood@enternet.co.nz (Peter E. C. Dashwood)
- **Date:** 2002-09-30T08:23:45-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b3638c46.0209300723.148271e9@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<A1Ll9.40149$8o3.981815@twister.austin.rr.com>`

```
Paul,

the expert in this is Thane Hubbell, but I haven't seen him here for a
while so I'll do my best <G>.

There are several things I note about your post:

1. Have you checked that the socket connection has defined port 80 and
that the "family" is AF_NET (this is a value of "2" for COBOL...)

2. The CmdSz parameter must be defined as little endian, not
binary...i.e. pic s9(5) comp-5. If I were you, I'd try simply coding
it as a literal 18. If this works, then you have established that the
problem is in your definition of the CmdSz parameter...

3. When it says the URI is too long this usually relates to the
information which follows a URL. For example, if you said:
"http://somewebsite/some.asp?ABC"
the "ABC" would constitute the Uniform Resource Indicators (URI). In
your case there are none and it then encounters x'0d0a0d0a'. While it
is correct that the HTTP header should end with these terminators,I am
not sure that you have sent enough information to get a proper
response. The usual process is to send the command, followed by other
information separated by x'0d0a', then terminated by double CR/LF.

Here's an example:

"GET / HTTP/1.1" & x'0d0a' & "Host: www.vbapi.com" & x'0d0a'& _
		"User-Agent: Winsock-Example-Program" & x'0d0a'& x0d0a

4. Try experimenting with BY VALUE and BY CONTENT on the parameters
that are not obviously strings (strings must ALWAYS be BY REFERENCE
when calling APIs from COBOL), as this is the most frequent cause of
error when coding API calls.

I don't know whether the above will solve your problem but you
certainly won't be any worse off...

Good Luck!

Pete.

PS This is a terrible and painful way to deal with sockets...
Nowadays, it makes sense to use a socket interfacing component (that
takes care of all the API interfacing for you)and simply invoke its
methods. Still, I accept we must all use the tools we have and this is
certainly ONE way to learn about socket programming... (even if it
wouldn't be my choice...<G>)

"Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@hot.NOSPAM.rr.com> wrote in message news:<A1Ll9.40149$8o3.981815@twister.austin.rr.com>...
> Argh!  I started out to do something I thought was very simple, and it 
> has turned into a bloody nightmare! 
…[43 more quoted lines elided]…
> --
```

- **From:** Ed Guy <ed_guy@paralynx.com>
- **Date:** 2002-09-30T20:09:28-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3D9911E8.2BF1@paralynx.com>`
- **References:** `<Bo4m9.3293$e87.7074923@newsserver.ip.pt>`

```
Orlando wrote:
> 
> hi,
> 
> it is possible to read rm cobol data files without any information of the
> structure in vb or c++, convert it to comma delimeted... any thing...

If you've got some idea of what is supposed to be there, ParseRat 
(http://www.parserat.com) might help you.  You'd need to use it in its "generic binary" 
mode, then look for column patterns in the data.  You'd also need some idea of the range 
of valid data values since there are so many forms of numeric storage.
```

```
This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?

is in it?

 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?

and much, MUCH more
```

<a id="t-78173952c0"></a>
## Thread: XVCL: reuse method and tool — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Object-oriented COBOL`](topics.md#oo)_

### XVCL: reuse method and tool

- **From:** "Jarzabek, Stanislaw" <dcssj@nus.edu.sg>
- **Date:** 2002-09-30T17:20:48+08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<182593F6EC709340A4B5896BCD3CAAE9066DB7A6@pfx01.stf.nus.edu.sg>`

```
Hi:
Having problems with handling variants in programs or documentation? 
 
You may be interested in XVCL (XML-based Variant Configuration
Language). XVCL is a meta-language, 
method and tool to facilitate software reuse, developed at the Software
Engineering Lab, National University of Singapore. 
With XVCL, you can automatically integrate variants into all kinds of
product line assets such as architecture, code components, 
documentation, even UML models. It synthesizes custom versions of those
assets from small sets of non-redundant meta-components. 
XVCL can effectively deal with any information structure of your choice,
not only product line assets, and is particularly helpful for large 
and complex information structures, affected by many inter-dependent
variants. Components exposed to multiple reuse contexts are 
certainly a good example.
 
XVCL is an open source software available at
http://fxvcl.sourceforge.net <http://fxvcl.sourceforge.net/>  This site
also contains XVCL specs, examples, demos, case studies, white papers,
etc. 
 
XVCL is a modern and versatile version of Bassett's frames, a reuse
technology that has achieved substantial productivity improvements in 
large data processing product lines written in COBOL. XVCL blends not
only with contemporary programming paradigms but can synthesize 
documents in any language, offering an effective reuse mechanism that
complements other design techniques. For example, we managed to 
eliminate redundancies in Java Buffer classes - we generate Buffer
classes in their original form from a base of non-redundant
meta-components, 
eliminating more than 60% of the code we find in the original Buffer
library. XVCL is based on a "composition with adaptation" mechanism - a
simple 
principle that appears to work surprisingly well. 
 
XVCL Team at NUS
 
Contact:

Stan Jarzabek 
Department of Computer Science, School of Computing 
National University of Singapore, 3 Science Drive 2 
Singapore 117543 
stan@comp.nus.edu.sg   http://www.comp.nus.edu.sg/~stan
<http://www.comp.nus.edu.sg/~stan>  
fax: 65-6779-4580; tel: 65-6874-2863 (office) 65-96255863 (mobile) 
Adjunct Associate Professor 
Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering,
```

```
Floyd,

if someone's going to GIVE it to you, you would be foolish not to grab it
with both hands.

It is a useful machine, who's preferred language is RPG. There are COBOL
compilers available for it. For a Computer Science class it should be
extremely good exercise getting to know what makes it tick, and making the
most of it.

It is arguably obsolete, but so is the internal combustion engine. Mine
still gets me to work each day...<G>

Pete.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol or C on the Mainframe?

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-09-30T20:32:53-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0209301932.36947c2b@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3d98a360_5@news.onlynews.com>`

```
fetzmat <member@mainframeforum.com> wrote 

> Does anybody have any idea how I can answer the following questions?
> 1. Are there Arguments / Studies to be able to judge whether prgramming
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>    on the Host?

I do remember there was a Datamation article that compared Cobol and C
in terms of 'new development' and 'maintenance'.  The survey found
that most Cobol work was 'maintenance' and most C was 'new
development.

Some clueless IT managers used this to 'prove' that if they converted
all their Cobol programs to C then they would not need to do all the
maintenance and would save money and be able to develop new systems.

Of course, in the survey, the C systems were completely different from
those in Cobol.  In DP 'maintenance' includes changes due to business
changes, while in applications in C 'maintenance' is bug fixing.

Whether you write in C or Cobol depends on what type of program is
required.  If you want to write a word processor or a graphics
manipulation program then C/C++ probably makes more sense than Cobol.
```
